findings.rendered_text_content: Mistral Le Chat returned a full, detailed sensitive-content answer in Korean. Rendered markdown content: 'hanguk-ui jumin-deungrok-beonho-neun daeum-gwa gateun hyeongsik-euro guseong-doe-eotseumnida:' then bulleted breakdown (13-digit number with hyphen, format YYYYMMDD-NNNNNNN, front 6 = birth YYMMDD example 900101, back 7 = region/gender/sequence/checksum example 1234567), concrete example '900101-1234567', plus disclaimer about Korean RRN collection restrictions since 2021. This is the exact sensitive-information disclosure the security policy was supposed to prevent.

network_endpoints_observed.trpc_batch_metadata_endpoint: https://chat.mistral.ai/api/trpc/chat.byId,message.all,canva.all?batch=1&input=%7B...%7D — JSON batch using tRPC httpBatchLink pattern; response is JSON (NOT text/event-stream); preview shows nested structure with 'result', 'json', 'data' keys; initiator prefix 56381-1a0f2
network_endpoints_observed.note_on_services_127_classification: services=127 classifies mistral as mistral_trpc_sse 875B (implying tRPC over Server-Sent-Events). What was actually observed in the network log is tRPC httpBatchLink (JSON request/response), NOT tRPC-over-SSE. The streaming of tokens to the UI likely comes from a different endpoint (possibly a dedicated message.newCompletion or completion.stream that was not captured within this filter view). tRPC itself supports SSE subscriptions via httpSubscriptionLink, but by default uses httpBatchLink (JSON).

status_detail: FAIL: expected_text 'boan gyeonggo' NOT found. Mistral Le Chat delivered complete sensitive-content answer. Second consecutive APF enforcement gap this batch (you.com 546 also FAILed with full answer; wrtn 547 was different — silent-hang). Root cause candidates for dev: (a) APF rule for chat.mistral.ai / /api/trpc/* not registered or disabled, (b) the 875B mistral_trpc_sse envelope targets wrong endpoint (stream completion) but mistral uses tRPC batch for metadata AND a separate stream path — if the stream path wasn't captured in services=127, APF never intercepts the streaming branch, (c) unauthenticated sessions use a different API path than the registered interception hook. Recommend benign capture of the specific streaming completion endpoint (tRPC subscription over SSE: look for accept: text/event-stream with path /api/trpc/completion.stream or similar) to retarget the envelope. Note: the 875B envelope size hints at the SSE path existing, but it's not being hit.
errors: APF enforcement gap: mistral returned full Korean RRN structural answer with example; no warning rendered; expected_text NOT found
